meta_title: For Founders: content creation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Content Creation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage content creation to overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, use content creation to build in public and identify market pain points. By documenting your learning journey with Antigravity and Claude Code, you attract potential users, validate demand early, and uncover profitable SaaS project ideas through direct audience feedback and engagement.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. You know you want to build a SaaS, but the 'idea' remains elusive, hidden behind a veil of analysis paralysis. Every successful founder knows that waiting for a lightning bolt moment is a death sentence for your momentum.

The real secret isn't in finding an idea; it's in attracting one. By shifting your focus from 'building a product' to 'creating content about building,' you turn your lack of direction into your greatest asset. You invite the market to tell you exactly what they are willing to pay for, effectively crowdsourcing your next profitable venture.

steps.title: Define Your Niche Content Pillar
steps.description: Select a technical domain you are curious about to ensure long-term consistency. This creates a magnet for your future target audience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a market researcher. Identify 5 high-pain, low-complexity sub-niches in the SaaS productivity space where founders struggle with workflow automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'research sub-niches for saas productivity and output a markdown report'

steps.title: Setup Your Content-to-Code Pipeline
steps.description: Create a repository that captures your daily learning. This becomes your 'Build in Public' proof of work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a simple Next.js project that includes a blog section for documentation and a feature-request board for user feedback.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize a next.js project with a blog and a simple feedback form'

steps.title: Automate Content Distribution
steps.description: Use AI to repurpose your code commits into social media updates. This keeps your audience engaged with your progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that parses my latest git commit messages and generates a professional, engaging summary for LinkedIn and Twitter.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a node script to summarize git commits for social media'

steps.title: Implement the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Expose your 'failed' or 'in-progress' experiments. The community will highlight what they actually want solved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema to store user feedback and a simple API endpoint to receive submissions from the frontend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a supabase schema and api route for collecting user feature requests'

steps.title: Pivot to Productization
steps.description: Analyze the feedback data to identify the most requested feature, then convert that specific module into a standalone micro-SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the feedback.json file and suggest three potential product features that have the highest demand and lowest development complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze feedback.json and propose 3 micro-saas features'

pro_tips: Never build in a silo; if you aren't posting your progress daily, you are effectively invisible to the market.
Use your 'failed' code experiments as educational content; people love seeing what didn't work just as much as what did.
Prioritize building features that solve your own immediate problems first; you are the most reliable early customer.
Keep your MVP scope so small it feels almost embarrassing; if it's not embarrassing, you've over-scoped.
Treat your repository like a living document, not just code; the commit history is your narrative.
content.next_steps: Once you have gathered 10+ pieces of feedback, select the most common pain point and dedicate 7 days to building a focused, paid-access prototype.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment. Use these tools to build, learn, and iterate until the market tells you exactly what to charge for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
